So all of our forums today are working through HubSpot.

So whether that be this forum, whether that be any of the forums that you see on our gated landing page, pages, I should say, whether that be any of the gated
content that flows from guides or blogs or anywhere else, all flows through HubSpot.
HubSpot then ingests the lead, and then every lead is assigned a lead score.
Depending on how much behavior in that person's profile, it'll either trigger an MQL or not trigger an MQL.
That MQL then gets routed to the appropriate account, the prospecting owner, I should say.
That prospecting owner then gets an alert.
They have an SLA as to when they need to follow up.
That follow-up time depends on the nature of the MQL.
If it is a hand-raiser MQL, like a demo request like we see here on the screen, or if it's a content lead, they have different options.

The content leads have an eight-hour SLA, the demo requests have a one-hour SLA, and then if they miss the SLA, then their manager gets banged and they get hounded to follow up or they get the lead rerouted to a new person in the event that person is out of office or, you know, for whatever reason.
And then that's pretty much it.
That's kind of the main part of the upfront of the process.

I did flag the Check.Team at our company to ask a very important question around ChatGPT and how we're pulling data from ChatGPT, because there are two ways.
We can turn web search on, which turns on citations by default, which is what Scrunch does.
And that inflates visibility because it's going to constantly be referencing different websites.
Based on my analysis so far of the prompt responses in Check.That, we're not doing that.
So we are prompting ChatGPT the way most users do, which is they don't turn on web search.
